meta.source: MTU ECU 8 / ECU 9 SAE J1939 Functional Description
meta.note: J1939 communication interface spec. Editable config is limited to Section 5; command PGNs are controllable; telemetry PGNs are read-only. Deep engine-tuning values (governor/fuel/droop curves) live in a separate MTU parameter-list manual.

config_params.name: J1939 Node Number (ECU)
config_params.description: CAN source address the ECU communicates under on the J1939 bus. Set via DiaSys; takes effect after restart.

config_params.name: J1939 External Controller Node Number
config_params.description: Node number the ECU uses to receive messages from the external/generator controller (third-party controller).

config_params.name: CAN Interface Configuration
config_params.description: When J1939 is active, the PCS-5 protocol must be set Inactive (0) or Automatic Transmission (66).

config_params.name: J1939 Status
config_params.description: Master enable for the J1939 protocol. 0 = Off, 1 = On.

config_params.name: Speed Dependent Transmission Time
config_params.description: Time difference applied when transmitting a J1939 message depending on engine speed.

config_params.name: J1939 Generator Controller Node Number
config_params.description: Node number the ECU receives generator-controller messages from. (ECU 8 only.)

config_params.name: DM1 Delete
config_params.description: Allows active faults (DM1) to be deleted with DM3 (0) or DM11 (1) for downward compatibility. (ECU 8 only.)

config_params.name: TSC 1 Cycle Time
config_params.description: Transmission cycle time for the TSC1 torque/speed control message. (ECU 9 only.)
